description: Configure GPredict for a bistatic radar bounce experiment listening post. Create a ground station named 'Strasbourg_RX' (48.5839 N, 7.7455 E, 140 meters altitude) and set it as the default ground station. Update the global minimum pass elevation to 25 degrees to filter out low-horizon geometry. Create a tracking module named 'High_RCS_Targets' using a multi-pane 'Map, Table, and Polar' layout. Add the following satellites to the module: ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544), CSS (TIANHE) (NORAD 48274), METOP-B (NORAD 38771), and METOP-C (NORAD 43689). Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I need you to set up GPredict for our radar forward-scatter experiment. We're listening for echoes from the GRAVES radar bouncing off massive satellites. First, add our listening post as a ground station: call it Strasbourg_RX, located at 48.5839 North, 7.7455 East, 140 meters altitude. Make sure to set it as the default ground station in the app preferences. Since we need good geometry for radar bounces, go into the preferences and change the minimum elevation for passes to 25 degrees. Next, create a new tracking module called High_RCS_Targets. When creating it, change its layout from the basic list to the 'Map, Table, and Polar' view so we can see all the data at once. Finally, add these four massive satellites to it: the ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544), the Chinese space station core CSS (TIANHE) (NORAD 48274), and two huge European weather satellites, METOP-B (NORAD 38771) and METOP-C (NORAD 43689).
